records.ety: [L. criminalis, fr. crimen: cf. F. criminel. See Crime.]

quotes: The neglect of any of the relative duties renders us criminal in the sight of God.

defs: Involving a crime; of the nature of a crime; -- said of an act or of conduct; as, criminal carelessness.
quotes: Foppish and fantastic ornaments are only indications of vice, not criminal in themselves.

defs: Relating to crime; -- opposed to civil; as, the criminal code.
quotes: The officers and servants of the crown, violating the personal liberty, or other right of the subject . . . were in some cases liable to criminal process.

defs: One who has committed a crime; especially, one who is found guilty by verdict, confession, or proof; a malefactor; a felon.
